data.title: Burnout Memoirs Reflect Traditional Addiction Narratives
data.description: A new wave of burnout memoirs is emerging, drawing parallels to traditional addiction stories with vivid details and intense lows. Bloomberg posted on X, these narratives replace tales of wild nights with accounts of solitary all-nighters and anxiety-filled moments at the desk. The memoirs delve into the personal experiences of individuals grappling with burnout, highlighting the emotional and physical toll it takes. This trend underscores the growing recognition of burnout as a significant issue in modern work culture, prompting discussions about mental health and work-life balance.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | U.S. Secretary of State Rubio Visits Hungary for Nuclear Cooperation Talks
data.description: U.S. Secretary of State Rubio arrived in Budapest on Monday to meet with Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban and his government. According to Jin10, the discussions centered around a civilian nuclear energy cooperation agreement strongly advocated by U.S. President Donald Trump. The agreement aims to enhance bilateral relations and promote energy collaboration between the two nations.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Iran's Revolutionary Guard Conducts Naval Drills in Strait of Hormuz
data.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guard Navy has conducted military exercises in the Strait of Hormuz to assess its readiness against potential security and military threats. According to Jin10, the exercises are part of Iran's ongoing efforts to ensure its strategic capabilities in the region. The Strait of Hormuz is a critical waterway for global oil shipments, and any military activity in the area is closely monitored by international observers. The drills underscore Iran's focus on maintaining a strong defensive posture amid regional tensions.

data.title: Wells Fargo Customer Experiences Delayed Check Processing
data.description: A Wells Fargo customer faced a challenging situation after depositing a $10,000 check at an ATM without receiving a receipt. According to NS3.AI, the funds were unexpectedly put on hold, leaving the customer in uncertainty for weeks. Despite repeated attempts to resolve the issue, the bank did not provide any explanation for the delay. Eventually, Wells Fargo escalated the matter and released the funds approximately a month later, but the reasons for the prolonged hold remain undisclosed.

data.title: STOCKS | Analysts Increase Earnings Estimates for Software Stocks Amid AI Concerns
data.description: Analysts are raising their earnings forecasts for software stocks despite recent selloffs driven by concerns over AI disruption. Bloomberg posted on X that Goldman Sachs strategists have noted this trend, highlighting the resilience of the software sector in the face of technological challenges. The strategists believe that the potential of AI to transform industries is prompting analysts to adjust their expectations, even as market volatility persists. This adjustment reflects a broader optimism about the long-term impact of AI on software companies, suggesting that the sector may continue to thrive despite short-term fluctuations. The strategists emphasize that while AI presents challenges, it also offers significant opportunities for growth and innovation within the software industry. As analysts continue to revise their earnings estimates, the software sector remains a focal point for investors looking to capitalize on technological advancements.

data.title: A whale opened a 20x leveraged short position in BTC and increased its short position in ETH, currently showing a floating profit of $1.47 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a whale opened a 20x leveraged short position in BTC (position of 50 BTC) and increased its short position in ETH to 21,000 ETH, and is currently showing a floating profit of $1.47 million.

data.title: Harvard University disclosed that it established its first Ethereum ETF position in Q4 2025.
data.description: A recent filing by Harvard Management Company, the fund manager for Harvard University, reveals that it reduced its Bitcoin ETF holdings by approximately 21% in the fourth quarter of 2025, while simultaneously establishing its first Ethereum ETF position. Its total exposure to the two major crypto assets is approximately $352.6 million. As of December 31, 2025, Harvard held approximately 5.35 million shares of BlackRock's IBIT, valued at $265.8 million, a decrease of approximately 1.48 million shares from the previous quarter's 6.81 million shares. At the same time, it established a new position in the iShares Ethereum Trust, holding approximately 3.87 million shares, valued at approximately $86.8 million, marking the fund's first publicly disclosed allocation to Ethereum-related products. Despite the reduction, Bitcoin remains Harvard's largest publicly disclosed equity holding, exceeding its holdings in Alphabet, Microsoft, and Amazon. (The Block)

data.title: Crypto News Today: Crypto ETP Outflows Extend to Fourth Week as $3.7B Exits in Past Month, According to CoinShares
data.description: Global cryptocurrency exchange-traded products (ETPs) recorded a fourth consecutive week of net outflows, with $173 million withdrawn last week, bringing total redemptions over the past month to $3.74 billion, according to data from CoinShares.The latest figures mark a slowdown from earlier in the month, when weekly outflows peaked near $1.7 billion, but signal that selling pressure has moderated rather than reversed, said James Butterfill.U.S. leads outflows as overseas demand improvesFund flows showed a sharp regional split. U.S.-listed crypto products posted $403 million in weekly outflows, while Europe and Canada collectively attracted $230 million in inflows.Germany led non-U.S. inflows with $115 million, followed by Canada ($46.3 million) and Switzerland ($36.8 million), suggesting investor demand has shifted overseas even as U.S. investors reduced exposure.Trading activity cools as volatility fadesCoinShares data also showed a sharp decline in trading activity. Weekly ETP volumes fell to $27 billion, down from a record $63 billion the previous week, pointing to fading speculative intensity alongside weaker prices.Butterfill noted that flows were volatile within the week: early inflows of $575 million were followed by $853 million in outflows, likely driven by renewed price weakness. Sentiment stabilized toward the end of the week after softer-than-expected U.S. CPI data triggered $105 million in inflows on Friday.Bitcoin and Ether lead redemptions, altcoins show pockets of strengthBitcoin investment products accounted for the largest share of redemptions, with $133 million in outflows. Notably, short-Bitcoin products also saw $15.4 million in outflows over the past two weeks, a pattern CoinShares said is often observed near market bottoms.Ethereum funds recorded $85.1 million in outflows, extending a period of sustained pressure, while smaller products such as Hyperliquid shed around $1 million.In contrast, selective altcoins continued to attract capital. XRP led inflows with $33.4 million, followed by Solana at $31 million and Chainlink at $1.1 million.Market contextThe sustained outflows come amid subdued price action. Bitcoin has slipped nearly 2% over the past week and remains below $70,000, while Ether continues to trade under $2,000 after two weeks of heavy redemptions.While the pace of selling has slowed, CoinShares said the data point to a market still in retrenchment, with investors selectively reallocating rather than broadly re-entering crypto exposure, according to The Block.

data.title: Harvard Management Company Adjusts Cryptocurrency ETF Holdings
data.description: Harvard Management Company reduced its investment in Bitcoin ETFs by 21% during the fourth quarter compared to the previous quarter. According to NS3.AI, the endowment simultaneously increased its holdings by adding $86.8 million worth of Ethereum ETFs to its portfolio. Currently, the total value of Harvard's investments in BlackRock's IBIT (Bitcoin) and ETHA (Ethereum) ETFs stands at $352.6 million, with Bitcoin ETFs constituting the majority of these holdings.

data.title: Rosebank Nears Acquisition of Two U.S. Firms for $3.05 Billion
data.description: Rosebank is reportedly in the final stages of negotiations to acquire two U.S. companies for a combined total of $3.05 billion, including debt. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the strategic move as part of Rosebank's expansion efforts in the U.S. market. The acquisition is expected to enhance Rosebank's portfolio and strengthen its position in the industry. Details about the specific companies involved in the deal have not been disclosed. The transaction underscores Rosebank's commitment to growth and diversification in its business operations.

data.title: Egyptian Startup Breadfast Secures Funding from Global Investors
data.description: Egyptian startup Breadfast has successfully attracted investment from several prominent global entities in its latest financing round. Bloomberg posted on X that the $330 billion sovereign wealth fund of the United Arab Emirates, a Saudi billionaire family, and Japan’s SBI Investment are among the key backers. This funding round marks a significant milestone for Breadfast as it seeks to expand its operations and enhance its market presence. The involvement of these major investors underscores the growing interest in the Middle Eastern startup ecosystem and highlights Breadfast's potential for growth and innovation in the region.

data.title: Goldman Sachs Reaffirms Two Fed Rate Cuts This Year, Next Move Seen in June
data.description: Goldman Sachs said it continues to expect the U.S. Federal Reserve to cut interest rates twice this year, with the next reduction likely to come in June, as inflation pressures show signs of easing.Lindsay Rosner, head of multi-sector fixed income investing at Goldman Sachs Asset Management, said the January consumer price index (CPI) data was “not as strong as feared,” helping clarify the Fed’s path toward policy normalization, according to a report cited by Jin10.Rosner noted that the outlook remains closely tied to labor market conditions, emphasizing that the Federal Reserve and the Federal Open Market Committee are particularly sensitive to signs of employment weakness.“If the labor market continues to show improvement, the normalization path becomes clearer,” she said, adding that Goldman’s base case remains two rate cuts in 2026, with the next cut expected in June.The assessment aligns with broader market expectations that easing inflation will eventually allow the Fed to lower borrowing costs, even as policymakers remain cautious about declaring victory over price pressures.

data.title: U.S. CPI Data Supports Continued Fed Rate Cuts, but Distortions Cloud Inflation Signal
data.description: The latest U.S. consumer price index (CPI) data has reinforced expectations that the Federal Reserve can continue easing monetary policy, though analysts caution that recent data distortions may be masking underlying inflation trends.Adam Button, an analyst at InvestingLive, said market pricing shifted modestly in a dovish direction following the CPI release. The U.S. dollar weakened, while S&P 500 futures erased earlier losses, reflecting improved risk sentiment.Button noted that the CPI report was affected by data irregularities linked to the recent U.S. government shutdown. October CPI data was not released, and November price collection began later than usual, capturing a higher share of seasonal holiday discounts. Economists have warned that these factors may have artificially suppressed the headline inflation reading.Food inflation stands outDespite the softer overall CPI print, food prices—particularly meat—showed notable strength. Meat prices rose 8.9% year-on-year, the largest increase since 2022, while fresh ground beef prices surged nearly 15%, emerging as a key inflation hotspot within the report.Policy implicationsWhile the lower-than-expected CPI data was welcomed by markets and supports the case for the Federal Reserve to continue cutting interest rates, analysts stressed the need for caution. Button said the December CPI report is likely to provide a clearer view of underlying inflation dynamics, once temporary distortions fade.For now, the data has eased near-term inflation concerns and strengthened expectations for continued policy easing, even as uncertainty remains over how quickly price pressures are truly cooling.

data.title: Imran Khan Seeks Release Due to Health Concerns
data.description: Former Prime Minister of Pakistan, Imran Khan, is advocating for his release from custody, highlighting concerns over his worsening eye condition. Bloomberg posted on X, detailing Khan's appeal for medical attention and his request for freedom based on health grounds. Khan's legal team is actively pursuing his release, emphasizing the urgency of his medical needs. The situation has drawn attention to the conditions of his detention and the impact on his health. Khan's supporters are rallying for his release, underscoring the significance of his health issues in their campaign.

data.title: Crypto Market Rallies Ahead of Lunar New Year Amid Volatility
data.description: The cryptocurrency market is witnessing a rally as the Lunar New Year approaches, driven by an oversold rebound. According to NS3.AI, investors are advised to exercise rational judgment in the face of ongoing volatility. The article emphasizes the importance of caution when considering reducing positions at market highs to manage risk effectively.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | BRICS Plans Precious Metals Exchange to Enhance Trade Autonomy
data.description: BRICS member countries are advancing plans to create a dedicated Precious Metals Exchange within special economic zones. According to NS3.AI, this initiative aims to promote independent pricing and reduce reliance on Western financial systems. The move aligns with the ongoing deployment of a BRICS gold-backed currency pilot, designed to enhance trade autonomy and bypass dollar-centric mechanisms. Officials anticipate the exchange will become operational around 2030, facilitating the trade of metals and rare commodities while fostering fair competition among member states.

data.title: Train Derailment in Southern Switzerland Causes Disruptions
data.description:
A train derailment occurred in southern Switzerland, with no immediate reports of injuries. According to RTHK, Swiss police announced that the incident took place at 7 a.m. local time on Monday when a train derailed near Goppenstein in the canton of Valais. Authorities have not provided further details, but injuries have not been ruled out.

The Swiss Federal Railways operator stated on its website that train services in the Goppenstein area will be affected due to an avalanche. The situation is being monitored, and updates will be provided as more information becomes available.

data.title: CoinShares: Global crypto ETPs saw a net outflow of $3.74 billion over the past month, marking the fourth consecutive week of net outflows.
data.description: A recent report from CoinShares shows that global crypto asset investment products saw an outflow of $173 million last week, bringing the cumulative outflow over the past four weeks to $3.74 billion. James Butterfill, Head of Research at CoinShares, stated that the latest data indicates selling pressure has eased but not yet reversed. The week began with an inflow of $575 million, followed by an outflow of $853 million. On Friday, a $105 million inflow occurred due to lower-than-expected CPI data. Trading activity declined significantly this week, with ETP trading volume falling from a record $63 billion last week to $27 billion. Regionally, US digital asset funds saw an outflow of $403 million, while other regions saw inflows of $230 million. Germany led the way with $115 million in inflows, followed by Canada with $46.3 million and Switzerland with $36.8 million. In terms of specific assets, Bitcoin investment products saw an outflow of $133 million, while products shorting Bitcoin experienced a cumulative outflow of $15.4 million over the past two weeks. Ethereum-related funds saw an outflow of $85.1 million, and smaller products like Hyperliquid saw outflows of $1 million. In contrast, some altcoins recorded inflows: XRP saw an inflow of $33.4 million, Solana $31 million, and Chainlink $1.1 million. Currently, the price of Bitcoin remains below $70,000, and Ether, after two weeks of large-scale outflows, is trading below $2,000. (The Block)

data.title: French Company Capital B Increases Bitcoin Holdings
data.description: French publicly traded company Capital B has announced an increase in its Bitcoin holdings by 6 BTC. According to Odaily, this brings the company's total Bitcoin reserves to 2,834 BTC. Capital B is now ranked 28th among publicly listed companies with Bitcoin treasuries.

data.title: Elon Musk Endorses Solar Power as Future Dominant Energy Source
data.description: Elon Musk has publicly endorsed solar power as the dominant future source of electricity, emphasizing its anticipated growth in the United States. According to NS3.AI, solar capacity is expected to increase significantly in 2026–2027, with a projected 49% rise in operating capacity and plans to surpass coal. This optimism has led to a surge in clean energy stocks, particularly those focused on solar energy, despite economic challenges such as inflation and subsidy reductions.
